summary: Matrix mix delivers crystal‑clear sound that even beats the 30 Trips box, and the Lakeland show shines with tight sets and standout jams.
review: Both reviewers rave about the audio fidelity – one calling it better than the legendary 30 Trips release – while describing a lively two‑set performance packed with classics like Jack Straw, Peggy‑O, Looks Like Rain and a soaring Terrapin > Space sequence. The mix is praised as crisp and well‑balanced, and even the reviewer who gave zero stars notes “Great Sound!” and glowing vocal work from Garcia.

ai_rating.rationale: Strong positive comments on both sound quality (matrix mix, superior to official release) and musical performance outweigh the anomalous zero‑star rating; limited to two reviews gives medium confidence.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crisp, detailed mix praised as better than the 30 Trips box set; no noted dropouts or muddiness.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Two energetic sets with concise first‑set tunes (most under 10 min) that build into a second set featuring the Terrapin > Space jam and a joyous close with Sugar Magnolia.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Vocals described as shining; guitar work noted as “shreds”.
band_member_comments.Phil: Mentioned in the “Passenger‑Phil Zone” comment, implying solid bass presence.
band_member_comments.Drums: Described as “Super Sized”, indicating a powerful drum sound.
